30		// error the upstream surfaced — notably agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible
31		// when the harness allow-list rejects the model. Any non-nil error
32		// causes the loop to exit immediately without claiming the bead, with
33		// a worker-level execution_failed record naming the rejected pair.
34		// DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list; this gate only

46	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
47	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
48	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
49	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
50	
51	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
52	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
53	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
54	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
55	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
56	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
57	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
58	
59	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
60	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
61	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
62	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
63	// advance the base branch.
64	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
65	
66	// capLoopCooldown clamps a loop-set cooldown duration to MaxLoopCooldown.
67	// The loop uses this for every SetExecutionCooldown call so no automatic
68	// path can silently park a bead for a year.
69	func capLoopCooldown(d time.Duration) time.Duration {
70		if d <= 0 {
71			return 0
72		}
73		if d > MaxLoopCooldown {
74			return MaxLoopCooldown
75		}
76		return d
77	}

1	# DDx Agent Execute: Operator Guide
2	
3	This is the short operator reference for `ddx agent execute-loop` and
4	`ddx agent execute-bead`. For the full flag list, see `ddx agent execute-loop
5	--help` and `ddx agent execute-bead --help`.
6	
7	## Which command do I run?
8	
9	- **`ddx agent execute-loop`** — the primary surface. Drains the project's
10	  execution-ready bead queue. Claims the next ready bead, runs
11	  `execute-bead` on it, records the result, and moves on until no
12	  unattempted ready work remains. Reach for this by default.
13	- **`ddx agent execute-bead <id>`** — the primitive. Runs one agent on one
14	  bead in an isolated worktree. Use it for debugging, replaying, or
15	  re-running a specific bead. The loop calls this internally for every
16	  attempt.
17	
18	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets. Any bead
19	whose dependencies are met and whose acceptance criteria still require
20	work is eligible — the agent produces whatever artifacts the acceptance
21	says to produce (docs, specs, code).
22	
23	## Result statuses
24	
25	Every execute-bead attempt reports one of these statuses on its `status:`
26	line (and in the JSON result). The loop's close/unclaim behavior is
27	determined entirely by this value.
28	
29	| Status                         | Meaning                                          | Loop action                                             |
30	|--------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
31	| `success`                      | Agent produced changes; merged (or preserved with `--no-merge`) | Close bead with session + commit evidence |
32	| `already_satisfied`            | Bead returned `no_changes` on repeated attempts  | Close bead with accumulated no-changes evidence         |
33	| `no_changes`                   | Agent ran but produced no diff                   | Unclaim; may apply cooldown; close after N attempts     |
34	| `land_conflict`                | Rebase/merge failed; result preserved under `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/...` | Unclaim; leave bead open                    |
35	| `post_run_check_failed`        | Post-run checks failed; result preserved         | Unclaim; leave bead open                                |
36	| `execution_failed`             | Agent or harness error                           | Unclaim; leave bead open                                |
37	| `structural_validation_failed` | Bead or prompt inputs invalid                    | Unclaim; leave bead open                                |
38	
39	**Rule of thumb:** only `success` and `already_satisfied` close the bead.
40	Every other status leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt
41	can try again.
42	
43	## What the loop records per attempt
44	
45	Each attempt appends one `execute-bead` event to the bead with:
46	
47	- `status` — one of the values above
48	- `detail` — human-readable reason
49	- `base_rev` — git rev the attempt started from
50	- `result_rev` — git rev of the resulting commit (when present)

50	- `result_rev` — git rev of the resulting commit (when present)
51	- `preserve_ref` — `refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<ts>-<shortsha>` (when preserved)
52	- `retry_after` — RFC3339 time for no-progress cooldowns
53	
54	The underlying agent session log for the attempt is written via the
55	execute-bead agent-log path (see `ddx agent log`), so session IDs on
56	closed beads can be replayed.
57	
58	## Common operations
59	
60	```bash
61	# Drain the current ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
62	ddx agent execute-loop
63	
64	# Process at most one ready bead and stop
65	ddx agent execute-loop --once
66	
67	# Run as a long-lived worker
68	ddx agent execute-loop --poll-interval 30s
69	
70	# Run inline in the current process (no server worker)
71	ddx agent execute-loop --local --once
72	
73	# Debug a specific bead (primitive; bypasses the queue)
74	ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id>
75	
76	# Preserve the result instead of merging it back
77	ddx agent execute-bead <bead-id> --no-merge
78	```
79	
80	## Related
81	
82	- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — canonical close-semantics source
83	- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` — status constants
84	- `ddx agent execute-loop --help`, `ddx agent execute-bead --help`

input.new_string:
## Loop termination conditions

The execute-loop exits the `for` iteration only when one of these conditions holds:

| Condition | Details |
|---|---|
| Context cancelled | `ctx.Err() != nil` — e.g. operator `^C`, `ddx agent stop`, or server shutdown. Terminates immediately. |
| `Once == true` | Loop processed one bead (or exhausted the queue once) and exits. Set by `--once`. |
| `PollInterval <= 0` AND no ready candidate | Queue is empty (or all remaining candidates were already attempted this run) and the loop is not polling. Default mode. |
| `RoutePreflight` rejected | The configured harness/model pair was rejected by the upstream route table. Exits with an `execution_failed` record. |

**Store errors during outcome handling do NOT terminate the loop.** If a `Store.*` call (`Unclaim`, `CloseWithEvidence`, `Reopen`, `SetExecutionCooldown`, `AppendEvent`, etc.) fails after the executor returns, the loop logs the error, records a `kind:loop-error` event on the bead (best-effort), applies a short cooldown (`StoreErrorCooldown = 5m`) so the bead is not immediately re-queued, and continues to the next candidate. The worker exits only when `ctx` is cancelled.

**Pre-claim hook failures are NOT terminal.** A hook failure on the first presentation of a bead allows one retry: the bead is not added to `attempted` until the hook passes (or fails a second time). If the hook always fails for a bead (e.g. branch is persistently diverged), the bead is moved to `attempted` on its second failure so the loop can exit normally. Fix the divergence and restart the loop to process that bead.

## Related

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — canonical close-semantics source
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` — status constants
- `ddx agent execute-loop --help`, `ddx agent execute-bead --help`

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: execute-loop exits after one bead due to attempted tracking and fatal store errors [ddx-37cdb43a]

Root cause #1: attempted[candidate.ID] was set BEFORE the pre-claim hook ran.
A hook failure (e.g. diverged branch) left the bead in attempted so the next
nextCandidate call skipped it and the loop exited with zero beads processed.
Fix: use a hookFailed set to allow one retry per bead per run. The bead is
not moved to attempted until the hook passes (or fails a second time, preventing
an infinite spin when the hook always fails).

Root cause #2: every Store.* error in the post-Execute outcome block returned
from the loop immediately, killing the worker on any transient storage failure.
Fix: add handleOutcomeStoreError which logs the error, appends a kind:loop-error
event (best-effort), sets a StoreErrorCooldown (5m) on the bead, increments
result.Failures, and signals continue — only ctx cancellation is still terminal.

Tests: pre-claim hook retry, store error continues loop (table-driven, all
Store.* paths), and an e2e 3-bead drain guard.

Docs: add loop termination section to docs/agent-execute.md documenting the
only conditions that actually terminate the worker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

fix: execute-loop exits after one bead due to attempted tracking and fatal store errors [ddx-37cdb43a]

Root cause #1: attempted[candidate.ID] was set BEFORE the pre-claim hook ran.
A hook failure (e.g. diverged branch) left the bead in attempted so the next
nextCandidate call skipped it and the loop exited with zero beads processed.
Fix: use a hookFailed set to allow one retry per bead per run. The bead is
not moved to attempted until the hook passes (or fails a second time, preventing
an infinite spin when the hook always fails).

Root cause #2: every Store.* error in the post-Execute outcome block returned
from the loop immediately, killing the worker on any transient storage failure.
Fix: add handleOutcomeStoreError which logs the error, appends a kind:loop-error
event (best-effort), sets a StoreErrorCooldown (5m) on the bead, increments
result.Failures, and signals continue — only ctx cancellation is still terminal.

Tests: pre-claim hook retry, store error continues loop (table-driven, all
Store.* paths), and an e2e 3-bead drain guard.

Docs: add loop termination section to docs/agent-execute.md documenting the
only conditions that actually terminate the worker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

All hooks passed. The commit landed at `d57f1dd`. Let me do a final AC walkthrough:

**AC-1** ✅ `hookFailed` set introduced; `attempted[candidate.ID]` only set after hook passes (or on second hook failure). `TestExecuteBeadWorkerPreClaimHookFailureRetriesOnNextIteration` verifies the single-run retry; `TestExecuteBeadWorkerPreClaimHookAlwaysFailsLeavesBeadAvailable` verifies the always-fails case exits gracefully.

**AC-2** ✅ `handleOutcomeStoreError` converts all 16+ `return result, err` points in the outcome block to log + loop-error event + cooldown + continue. Context cancellation still terminates.

**AC-3** ✅ `TestExecuteBeadWorkerStoreErrorContinuesLoop` is table-driven over Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount, adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence, SetExecutionCooldown (multiple paths), and late AppendEvent. Each sub-test asserts (a) loop continues, (b) loop-error event recorded (except AppendEvent which is best-effort), (c) `result.Failures >= 1`.

**AC-4** ✅ `TestExecuteBeadWorkerEndToEndThreeBeadDrain` seeds 3 beads (no_changes, success, already_satisfied) and asserts all three are processed without premature exit.

**AC-5** ✅ All pre-existing tests pass unchanged; worker-state semantics not altered.

**AC-6** ✅ `docs/agent-execute.md` gained a "Loop termination conditions" section listing the four terminal conditions and explicitly stating Store errors and hook failures are non-terminal.

**AC-7** ✅ No "restart if exits early" guidance existed in the docs (searched exhaustively).
